---
description: "Use when: editing IELTS speaking content files, adding sourced study material, writing Dutch translations, or maintaining the topic catalog."
applyTo: "src/content/topics/*.md"
---

# Content Authoring Rules

1. Keep sourced material original but adapt if necessary. do not paste long passages from third-party sites. 
3. Use a fluent, natural style, but relevant for the IELTS speaking test. This means also a good use of linking words, variety of sentence structures, and clear expression of ideas.
4. Keep Dutch translations clear, idiomatic and learner-friendly.
5. Prefer 4 to 8 vocabulary items, 3 to 6 short questions, and 2 to 4 essay questions per topic.
